# Pr 23145 Adaptive Scratch And Deferred Ckpt Scheduler

Source type: github
Document ID: dsid_caf02469a41c46cf8387713b83c628f3
Release: v1.0.0
Benchmark: EnterpriseRAG-Bench synthetic upstream data
adaptive-scratch-capacity-and-deferred-ckpt-scheduler-for-gpu-workspace-reduction

Reduced GPU peak workspace by up to 28% on long-context models via adaptive scratch allocator and deferred activation checkpoint scheduling. No API changes. Rollout: opt-in via runtime flag 'adaptive_scratch=on' followed by gradual default enablement after smoke validation.
Motivation: GPU workspace (scratch) and activation memory are significant cost drivers for long-context generation and multi-tenant Dedicated pools. Previous changes (arena tuning, static checkpointing) helped but left headroom for per-request adaptation and better interaction with KV cache and continuous batching. This PR implements two coordinated changes: 1) An adaptive scratch allocator that shrinks kernel scratch reservations on hot paths using real allocation traces and a light-weight eviction policy; 2) A deferred activation checkpoint scheduler that opportunistically delays checkpoint insertion until after the kernel selection pass when sequence-to-sequence and KV cache usage are known. Design summary: The adaptive scratch allocator records per-model per-kernel high-water marks over rolling windows and exposes a soft cap that kernels consult at selection time; if the soft cap would be exceeded we attempt two actions: pick a lower-scratch kernel variant or apply a temporary micro-batch split. The deferred checkpoint scheduler moves most activation checkpointing decisions out of the eager graph transform and into the runtime scheduling stage so decisions can use real batch sizes, kv-cache presence, and current scratch headroom. This reduces over-checkpointing on requests that will hit the KV cache and reduces worst-case workspace pressure. Implementation notes: - runtime/inference/scratch_allocator.cpp: new AdaptiveScratchManager, hot-path lock-free counters, bounded trace buffer. - runtime/inference/checkpoint_scheduler.cpp: new DeferredCheckpointPlanner, heuristics to trade memory vs. compute (default: aim for <=15% additional compute). - runtime/kernels/kernel_selector.cpp: kernel variants now expose 'scratch_estimate' and 'checkpoint_safe' metadata; selection integrates with AdaptiveScratchManager. - runtime/scheduler/continuous_batcher.cc: minor changes to expose batching window to planner. - tests/perf/scratch_memory_bench.py: synthetic long-context scenarios across models (gpt-style, encoder-decoder) and KV cache toggles. - docs/ops/scratch-tuning.md: operational guidance and rollout recipe. Tests and results: microbenchmarks show peak GPU workspace reduction of 18-28% across 4 tested models at 32k context; tail latency impact is +4% median, +9% 95th when the allocator picks lower-scratch kernels; overall token throughput for large batches unchanged within noise. KV cache interplay: requests with warm KV cache see much larger benefits because deferred checkpointing avoids storing activations that are unnecessary for cached steps. Safety/rollout: feature gated behind runtime flag 'adaptive_scratch' and 'deferred_ckpt' (both default off). We added a gradual rollout plan: enable in Canary region, observe memory headroom and OOMs for 72h, then opt-in to Dedicated pools with autoscaler guards. Checklist: [x] unit tests, [x] perf scripts, [x] docs updated, [x] rollout plan, [x] security review (no secrets). Backwards compatibility: none; internal only. Related: ENG-3245 (workspace reductions initiative), ENG-3299 (kv-cache improvement spike).
Diego Ramos: This looks promising. Can you add a safety circuit for cases where the allocator oscillates and causes repeated micro-batch splits? Suggest a backoff cooldown per-model.
Maya Lin: Added a cooldown mechanism (AdaptiveScratchManager::cooldown_ms) and tested with aggressive allocation spikes. Benchmarks included in bench_results/2026-02-20.
Lucas Nguyen: The deferred checkpointing change is invasive. How does this interact with tiled-kv cache eviction? Please outline any assumptions about KV lifecycle.
Maya Lin: Deferred planner consults KVCache::is_warmed(request_id) during planning. If KV lifecycle indicates eviction in same window, we conservatively keep checkpoints. Documented in scratch-tuning.md.
Serena Park: CI failed on linux-gpu-lint initially, some style fixes needed in scratch_allocator.h.
Maya Lin: Fixed style nits and added clang-format CI enforcement for runtime/* files.
Merged to main after two rounds of review and CI stabilization. Feature gates left off by default; ops will enable in canary on 2026-02-25. No regressions observed in e2e smoke tests; memory headroom improved on Dedicated canary by ~22% on average.
